generated_from: Build DASHBOARD pages that build.json (the GitHub-integration FLOW) does NOT cover, gathered from scattered build-reference/archive pages @ 2026-06-07. WEAK BASELINE: all 4 shots are NEEDS_REACH and most are contrived single-build states; the stable builds LIST page and a plain build DETAIL page are NOT screenshotted anywhere in the docs (see settings[] for those routes). Routes confirmed from universe: builds list /accounts/[account]/projects/[project]/builds, build detail /builds/[buildId], compare /builds/compare and /builds/compare/[buildIdA]/[buildIdB]. THEME WARNING: compare-builds-modal is light (luminance 250, a form over the builds page); the other three read dark by luminance (compare-builds-view 101 borderline, 03-logs 16, 04-artifacts 27) BUT build LOG/terminal panels render dark even when the dashboard is in LIGHT mode, so these may actually be light-mode pages dominated by a dark log panel. Confirm each by eye on the first run and flip theme to match the page CHROME, not the log panel. PROJECT needs real builds; the Maestro shots (03-logs, 04-artifacts) came from an e2e tutorial build but any build's Logs/Artifacts will do (compare ignores content).
